commit.message:
fix(issues): Avoid supergroup refetches on row removal (#111865)

Reuse the previous supergroup request while the visible issue ids only
shrink. This keeps archive and resolve actions from issuing a new
supergroup lookup and flashing the stream back into a loading state.

commit.message:
Automerge: [mlir][Arith] Avoid sign overflow when narrowing signed operations (#189676)

Whether an arith operation can be truncated to a given bitwidth should
also depend on the sign semantics of the operation itself. Consider:
```
%input = /* upper bound > INT32_MAX, <= UINT32_MAX */ : index
%c0 = arith.constant 0 : index
%cmp = arith.cmpi sle, %input, %c0 : index
```

Previously, `checkTruncatability()` would correctly judge that only an
unsigned truncation could be legal, however the narrowing would still
proceed despite the fact that the `sle` predicate treated the MSB as the
sign.

Ensure that the sign is checked for signed comparison predicates and for
signed elementwise operations by enforcing a `CastKind::Signed`
restriction, whereby the narrowing patterns bail out on incompatible
input range/operation signedness.

**AI tooling usage disclaimer**
LIT tests were expanded from manual reproducer examples with LLM
assistance. Those additional test cases were verified to
regression-test, proofread and edited manually in accordance with the
"Human in the loop" policy. LLMs/generative tooling were not used for
implementation/documentation purposes.

---------

Signed-off-by: Artem Gindinson <gindinson@roofline.ai>
Co-authored-by: GPT 5.4 <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: arm-toolchain
repository.description: A project dedicated to building LLVM toolchain for Arm and AArch64 embedded targets and AArch64 native Linux development.

commit.message:
[mlir][Arith] Avoid sign overflow when narrowing signed operations (#189676)

Whether an arith operation can be truncated to a given bitwidth should
also depend on the sign semantics of the operation itself. Consider:
```
%input = /* upper bound > INT32_MAX, <= UINT32_MAX */ : index
%c0 = arith.constant 0 : index
%cmp = arith.cmpi sle, %input, %c0 : index
```

Previously, `checkTruncatability()` would correctly judge that only an
unsigned truncation could be legal, however the narrowing would still
proceed despite the fact that the `sle` predicate treated the MSB as the
sign.

Ensure that the sign is checked for signed comparison predicates and for
signed elementwise operations by enforcing a `CastKind::Signed`
restriction, whereby the narrowing patterns bail out on incompatible
input range/operation signedness.

**AI tooling usage disclaimer**
LIT tests were expanded from manual reproducer examples with LLM
assistance. Those additional test cases were verified to
regression-test, proofread and edited manually in accordance with the
"Human in the loop" policy. LLMs/generative tooling were not used for
implementation/documentation purposes.

commit.message:
fix(auth): use signInWithOtp for OTP resend to trigger Send Email Hook (#1001)

* fix(tests): handle prebuild-generated gradle.properties in SDK 55 test

expo prebuild adds newArchEnabled to android/gradle.properties, but
this file is gitignored. Skip the check when the file doesn't exist
and filter out prebuild-injected lines when it does.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(mobile-storefront): cast ctaLink to satisfy Expo Router typed routes

The HeroSlide ctaLink type expects Expo Router's route union, but
the slides come from dynamic config data as plain strings. Cast
to the expected type since the links are validated at runtime.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): add max_age=0 to force fresh OAuth session on retry

Jumia's /login endpoint detects prior sessions and redirects to
/connect/auth/callback with a stale session id, returning "no route
matched". Adding max_age=0 (OpenID Connect) forces Jumia to ignore
cached sessions and always present a fresh login page.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(auth): use signInWithOtp for OTP resend to trigger Send Email Hook

supabase.auth.resend() bypasses the custom Send Email Hook (send-auth-email
edge function → ZeptoMail), causing resend emails to silently fail since no
Supabase SMTP is configured. Replace with signInWithOtp() which properly
triggers the hook. Both verify screens already handle the OTP type fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(auth): address CodeRabbit review feedback

- Strengthen timer-blocked test: assert button exists and is disabled
- Use @/ path alias for test import
- Add navigation assertion after successful OTP verification
- Document OTP type:'email' vs type:'signup' fallback in comments

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(auth): address CodeRabbit round-2 review feedback

- Use Supabase { error } response shape in mocks instead of rejections
- Swap verify fallback order: try type:'email' first (matches signInWithOtp)
- Type RHF useController mock strictly, remove any casts
- Use aria-label + getByRole for OTP input (accessibility best practice)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
ci: add mypy config and enable check (#896)

- Add [tool.mypy] to pyproject.toml — ported from roboflow/supervision (strict=true, ignore_missing_imports=false, python_version="3.10", mypy_path="src"); add per-module ignore_missing_imports overrides for torchvision/pycocotools/timm/einops; add ignore_errors overrides for 54 pre-existing failing modules
- Add mirrors-mypy v1.19.1 hook to .pre-commit-config.yaml (after ruff, with --ignore-missing-imports for isolated env)
- Add .github/workflows/ci-typing.yml — runs mypy src/rfdetr/ on push/PR with CPU-only torch and full strict config from pyproject.toml
- Split typing dependencies into a dedicated group in `pyproject.toml`.
- Update `ci-typing.yml` to install only the typing group for type-checking.
- Remove `--ignore-missing-imports` from the pre-commit mypy hook configuration.

* Fix mypy typing issues across utility, dataset, inference, and training modules

commit.message:
feat(cli): ship Windows and GNU release targets (#16)

* feat(cli): ship Windows and GNU release targets

Summary:
- add Windows npm targets and GNU Linux release extras to the CLI
  packaging metadata and launcher fallback logic
- build real Windows self-host helper binaries in GitHub Actions and
  smoke test the packaged Windows tarballs on hosted runners
- enable packaged self-host runtime support on Windows with .exe
  resolution, shared process liveness checks, and stop markers

Rationale:
- the release pipeline only shipped the bundled self-host runtime for
  Unix targets, which left Windows builds without a usable helper
- we do not maintain a dedicated Windows builder, so GitHub Actions
  must produce and validate the Windows helper artifacts

Tests:
- turbo run check:fix
- bun run format
- bun run typecheck
- bun run lint --format json
- bun run test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* chore(repo): split server integration into manual workflow

Summary:
- remove the slow server integration lane from the default CI workflow
  and its required results gate
- add a dedicated manual-only server integration workflow that keeps
  the existing Postgres setup and integration test command

Rationale:
- the integration lane is materially slower than the normal PR checks
  and should not run automatically on every change
- moving it to a separate workflow keeps the coverage available when
  maintainers need it without blocking regular CI

Tests:
- actionlint .github/workflows/ci.yml .github/workflows/server-integration.yml

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Eject a possibly stale workspace cache so future mutations don't rely on it.

With `but commit` we noticed raciness, which is now handled by not only
getting a write-lock early on commit, but also by ensuring the workspace cache
is not stale after `init_ctx()` read it *before* changing the workspace materially.

Co-authored-by: GPT 5.4 <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(runtime): Capture execution failures in Sentry (#132)

Capture execution failures in Sentry

Junior currently logs `execution_failure` turns as warn-only
diagnostics, which is why the fallback response can show up in
production without a useful Sentry issue to inspect. This promotes those
turns to captured exceptions and attaches a short derived failure reason
to the event.

This is intentionally an observability-only change. It does not explain
or fix the underlying execution failure yet; it makes the next
production failure show up as actionable telemetry so we can investigate
it directly.

I also validated the linked `junior-prod` consumer with local `pnpm
build` runs in both repos.

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex GPT-5 <codex@openai.com>

chore(release): remove machine-specific windows test path

Summary:
- replace the tracked Administrator home path in the Windows runtime test
  fixture with a generic vault path
- keep the release safety preflight clear of machine-specific text while
  preserving the runtime-version assertions

Rationale:
- the local release dry-run correctly blocked on the tracked absolute path,
  so the repo needs a generic fixture before the release lane can proceed

Tests:
- node --test testing/native/device/windows/runtime-version.test.mjs
- rg -n "/Users/|/home/|[A-Za-z]:\\\\Users\\\\" --glob '!node_modules' \
  --glob '!dist' --glob '!coverage' .

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(chatview): preserve chronology and hosted continuations

Summary:
- keep reasoning, tool calls, and content in emitted order during live
  rendering and persisted reloads
- remap repeated hosted tool-call ids per assistant occurrence so managed
  continuations bind later tool results to the correct round
- add desktop automation coverage and autoresearch evidence for the
  saved-chat cutoff repro and provider-parity proof

Rationale:
- saved managed chats were collapsing chronology on reload and could end in
  blank assistant tool-use rounds with no final answer content
- the fix needs durable live repro helpers and regression locks so this lane
  stays verifiable instead of returning to ad hoc QA

Tests:
- bash autoresearch.sh
- AUTORESEARCH_RUN_ID=20260330T200230Z bash autoresearch.checks.sh
- node scripts/jest.mjs --config jest.config.cjs --runInBand \
  --runTestsByPath src/services/__tests__/SystemSculptService.test.ts
- node scripts/jest.mjs --config jest.config.cjs --runInBand \
  --runTestsByPath src/testing/automation/__tests__/DesktopAutomationBridge.test.ts
- node --test testing/native/desktop-automation/client.test.mjs
- node scripts/jest.mjs --config jest.config.cjs --runInBand \
  --runTestsByPath src/views/chatview/__tests__/input-handler-tool-loop.test.ts
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
release(plugin): prepare 5.3.2 candidate

Summary:
- preserve completed local Pi tool-call state in ChatView so Pi tool
  executions do not get rerouted through the hosted tool loop
- pin Studio Pi auth inventory to the bundled auth-storage path and
  expand macOS and Windows provider-connected coverage on
  openai/gpt-5.4-mini
- harden Windows native release prep with runtime-version refresh,
  redacted bootstrap diagnostics, updated release notes, and 5.3.2
  version metadata

Rationale:
- keep canonical Pi auth and tool-calling paths strict instead of
  masking breakage behind fallbacks
- make the Windows release lane reproducible and future-agent friendly
  so stale runtimes and transient host noise stop causing trial and
  error
- capture the actual 5.3.2 release evidence in-tree before any publish
  step

Tests:
- node --test testing/native/desktop-automation/runner.test.mjs
  testing/native/device/windows/bootstrap.test.mjs
  testing/native/device/windows/runtime-version.test.mjs
  testing/native/device/windows/run-desktop-automation.test.mjs
  testing/native/device/windows/run-clean-install-parity.test.mjs
  testing/native/device/windows/remote-run.test.mjs
- bash autoresearch.sh
- npm test
- npm run build
- npm run check:plugin
- SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_ID=openrouter
  SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_MODEL_ID=openai/gpt-5.4-mini
  SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_API_KEY="$OPENROUTER_API_KEY"
  npm run test:native:desktop:provider-connected
- SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_ID=openrouter
  SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_MODEL_ID=openai/gpt-5.4-mini
  SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_API_KEY="$OPENROUTER_API_KEY"
  npm run test:native:windows:provider-connected
- npm run check:release:native
- git diff --check
- gitleaks git . --log-opts='5.3.1..HEAD'

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
